title: lichen_util.c:21 lichen_sha256 NULL+0 handling inconsistent with TinyCrypt
description: Gap found: Round 1 missed API edge case. lichen_sha256() at line 21 allows input==NULL when inlen==0, intending to support 'hash nothing'. However, TinyCrypt's tc_sha256_update() rejects NULL data even with datalen==0 (it checks data==NULL before checking datalen==0). Result: lichen_sha256(NULL, 0, output) passes the EINVAL check but then fails with -EIO when TinyCrypt rejects it. This is inconsistent: caller expects success for zero-length input but gets I/O error. HOW TO FIX: Either (a) reject NULL input entirely (change line 21 to 'input == NULL' without the inlen check), or (b) handle zero-length specially before calling TinyCrypt. Very minor - unlikely to be called in practice.
close_reason: Fixed lichen_sha256 empty-input handling and added focused Zephyr regression test
